POS Lite
========
Simple form-based order entry for phone, LINE, walk-in, and other manual orders.
Creates invoice, stock picking, and printable receipt from a lightweight backend form.
    """,
    'author': 'AI-DEV-Module-Odoo17',
    'website': 'https://github.com/apcball/AI-DEV-Module-Odoo17',
    'license': 'LGPL-3',

        'security/security.xml',
        'security/ir.model.access.csv',
        'data/sequence_data.xml',
        'report/receipt_report.xml',
        'views/pos_order_view.xml',
        'views/pos_config_view.xml',
        'views/report_views.xml',
        'views/menu.xml',
        'wizard/payment_wizard_view.xml',
        'wizard/return_wizard_view.xml',
    ],
    'assets': {},
    'application': True,
    'installable': True,
    'auto_install': False,
}

# POS Lite Module for Odoo 17

**Lightweight form-based order entry for phone, LINE, and walk-in orders**

## 📋 Overview

POS Lite is a simplified Point-of-Sale module designed for manual order entry scenarios such as phone orders, LINE orders, and walk-in customers. Unlike the standard Odoo POS which uses a grid-based interface, POS Lite provides a clean, form-based interface that's easy to use on desktop and mobile devices.

## 🎯 Features

### Order Management
- **Multi-channel support**: Phone, LINE, Walk-in, Other
- **Customer information**: Name, phone, address, tax ID
- **Warehouse selection**: Per-order warehouse assignment
- **Pricelist support**: Automatic price calculation based on pricelist
- **Order lines**: Add products with quantity, price, discount
- **Automatic calculations**: Subtotal, tax, total, paid amount, change

### Payment Processing
- **Single payment per order**: Cash, Transfer, or Card
- **Payment wizard**: Easy payment registration
- **Journal selection**: Auto-select cash/bank journal from config

### Document Generation
- **Invoice creation**: Automatic `account.move` (out_invoice)
- **Stock picking**: Automatic `stock.picking` for product delivery
- **Receipt printing**: Three formats available
  - Thermal 58mm
  - Thermal 80mm
  - A4 PDF

### Security & Permissions
- **POS Lite User**: Can create/edit orders, register payments
- **POS Lite Manager**: Full access including configuration
- **Multi-company support**: Record rules for data isolation

## 🏗️ Module Structure

```
pos_lite/
├── __init__.py
├── __manifest__.py
├── README.md
├── data/
│   └── sequence_data.xml          # Order sequence configuration
├── models/
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── pos_order.py               # Main order model (pos.lite.order)
│   ├── pos_payment.py             # Payment model (pos.lite.payment)
│   ├── pos_config.py              # Configuration model (pos.lite.config)
│   ├── product_product.py         # Product search enhancement
│   └── res_partner.py             # Partner search enhancement
├── report/
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── receipt_report.py          # Receipt report model
│   └── receipt_report.xml         # Receipt templates & paper formats
├── security/
│   ├── ir.model.access.csv        # Access control lists
│   └── security.xml               # User groups & record rules
├── views/
│   ├── menu.xml                   # Menu items
│   ├── pos_order_view.xml         # Order form/tree/search views
│   └── pos_config_view.xml        # Configuration views
├── wizard/
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── payment_wizard.py          # Payment wizard model
│   └── payment_wizard_view.xml    # Payment wizard form
└── static/
    └── description/
        └── icon.png               # Module icon
```

## 📊 Data Models

### `pos.lite.order` (POS Lite Order)
Main order model with the following fields:

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| name | Char | Order number (auto-generated) |
| company_id | Many2one | Company |
| state | Selection | Draft → Paid → Done → Cancelled |
| channel | Selection | Phone, LINE, Walk-in, Other |
| customer_name | Char | Customer name (for walk-in) |
| partner_id | Many2one | Linked partner |
| partner_phone | Char | Customer phone |
| partner_address | Char | Customer address |
| partner_tax_id | Char | Customer tax ID |
| warehouse_id | Many2one | Warehouse |
| pricelist_id | Many2one | Pricelist |
| line_ids | One2many | Order lines |
| payment_ids | One2many | Payments |
| amount_untaxed | Monetary | Subtotal |
| amount_tax | Monetary | Tax amount |
| amount_total | Monetary | Total |
| amount_paid | Monetary | Paid amount |
| amount_residual | Monetary | Remaining balance |
| amount_change | Monetary | Change amount |
| invoice_id | Many2one | Created invoice |
| picking_id | Many2one | Created stock picking |

### `pos.lite.order.line` (Order Line)
| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| order_id | Many2one | Parent order |
| product_id | Many2one | Product |
| description | Char | Product description |
| qty | Float | Quantity |
| price_unit | Monetary | Unit price |
| discount | Float | Discount % |
| price_subtotal | Monetary | Subtotal (computed) |
| price_tax | Monetary | Tax (computed) |
| price_total | Monetary | Total (computed) |

### `pos.lite.payment` (Payment)
| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| order_id | Many2one | Parent order |
| payment_method | Selection | Cash, Transfer, Card |
| amount | Monetary | Payment amount |
| journal_id | Many2one | Account journal |
| note | Char | Payment note |

### `pos.lite.config` (Configuration)
| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| name | Char | Config name |
| company_id | Many2one | Company |
| warehouse_id | Many2one | Default warehouse |
| pricelist_id | Many2one | Default pricelist |
| journal_id | Many2one | Default payment journal |

## 🔄 Order Workflow

```
┌─────────┐    Register     ┌─────────┐    Process     ┌─────────┐
│  Draft  │ ───Payment───→ │  Paid   │ ───Order────→ │  Done   │
└─────────┘                 └─────────┘                 └─────────┘
     │                           │
     │                           │
     └───────────Cancel──────────┘
```

### States:
1. **Draft**: Initial state, can edit order lines and register payment
2. **Paid**: Payment received, can process to create invoice/picking
3. **Done**: Order completed, invoice and picking created
4. **Cancelled**: Order cancelled (cannot cancel if invoice posted or picking done)

## 🛡️ Security

### User Groups
- **POS Lite User** (`group_pos_lite_user`):
  - Create/Edit orders, order lines, payments
  - Read-only access to configuration
  - Cannot delete records

- **POS Lite Manager** (`group_pos_lite_manager`):
  - Full access to all models including configuration
  - Inherits User permissions

### Record Rules (Multi-company)
All models have company-based record rules:
- Orders: `[('company_id', 'in', company_ids)]`
- Order Lines: `[('company_id', 'in', company_ids)]`
- Payments: `[('company_id', 'in', company_ids)]`
- Config: `[('company_id', 'in', company_ids)]`

## 📦 Dependencies

```python
'depends': [
    'base',
    'mail',           # chatter/messaging
    'contacts',       # partner management
    'product',        # product catalog
    'stock',          # inventory/picking
    'account',        # invoicing
    'sale_management', # pricelists
]
```

## 🚀 Installation

1. Copy `pos_lite` folder to your Odoo addons path
2. Update module list in Odoo
3. Install "POS Lite" module
4. Configure default settings in POS Lite Config (optional)

## ⚙️ Configuration

### Default Settings (Optional)
Navigate to **POS Lite > Configuration** to set default:
- Warehouse
- Pricelist  
- Payment Journal

This allows faster order creation by pre-filling these fields.

### Sequence Configuration
Order numbers are auto-generated using the `pos.lite.order` sequence.
Default format: `POL00001`, `POL00002`, etc.

## 🖨️ Receipt Printing

Three receipt formats are available:

### Thermal 58mm
- Paper width: 58mm
- Margins: 2mm all sides
- DPI: 90
- Best for: Small thermal printers

### Thermal 80mm  
- Paper width: 80mm
- Margins: 3mm left/right, 2mm top/bottom
- DPI: 90
- Best for: Standard thermal printers

### A4
- Standard A4 paper
- Margins: 10mm left/right, 12mm top/bottom
- DPI: 90
- Best for: Printing on regular paper

## 📝 Usage Guide

### Creating an Order

1. Navigate to **POS Lite > Orders**
2. Click **Create**
3. Fill in:
   - **Channel**: Phone/LINE/Walk-in/Other
   - **Customer**: Select existing or enter name/phone
   - **Warehouse**: Select delivery warehouse
   - **Pricelist**: Select pricing
4. Add order lines:
   - Select product
   - Enter quantity
   - Price auto-fills from pricelist
   - Apply discount if needed
5. Click **Register Payment**
6. Enter payment amount and method
7. Click **Confirm**

### Processing the Order

1. Open the paid order
2. Click **Process Order**
3. System automatically:
   - Creates customer invoice
   - Posts the invoice
   - Creates stock picking
   - Validates the picking
4. Order moves to **Done** state

### Creating a Return

1. Open completed order (state = Done)
2. Click **Create Return**
3. Return wizard opens:
   - Select products to return
   - Enter return quantity (full or partial)
   - Add return reason (optional)
4. Click **Create Return**
5. System automatically:
   - Creates return order linked to original
   - Creates Credit Note (out_refund)
   - Creates incoming picking for stock return
   - Processes refund payment
6. Return order moves to **Done** state

### Return Features
- **Full return**: Return all items from original order
- **Partial return**: Return specific quantities
- **Cash refund**: Refund to cash/bank journal
- **Credit note**: Automatic credit note generation
- **Stock return**: Incoming picking for warehouse
- **Return tracking**: Track returned quantity per line

### Printing Receipt

1. Open completed order
2. Click receipt button (58mm/80mm/A4)
3. Print or save PDF

## 🔧 Technical Notes

### Model Naming
Models use `pos.lite.*` naming convention to avoid conflicts with standard Odoo POS module (`pos.order`, `pos.payment`, `pos.config`).

### Company Isolation
All models use `check_company=True` on relational fields and have record rules for multi-company data isolation.

### Performance
- Product and partner search enhancements are context-aware
- Only activates when `pos_lite_search` or `pos_lite_partner_search` context is set

## 📄 License

LGPL-3

## 👥 Authors

- AI-DEV-Module-Odoo17
- Website: https://github.com/apcball/AI-DEV-Module-Odoo17

synthesis:16:Odoo 17 frontend JavaScript development centers on the OWL (Odoo Web Library) UI framework, a component-based architecture that replaces legacy widget systems with modern reactive components, a service-based dependency injection layer, and a hook-driven lifecycle model — fundamentally reshaping how developers build interactive features in Odoo's web client. This synthesis consolidates the core architectural pillars, practical development patterns, and migration considerations that define frontend JavaScript work in Odoo 17.
synthesis:18:## OWL Framework and Component Model
synthesis:20:Odoo 17 continues its deep adoption of **OWL (Odoo Web Library)**, a custom UI framework inspired by React and Vue that uses a virtual DOM, reactive state management via `useState`, and a template system based on QWeb (XML-based templates). Every piece of UI in the Odoo web client — from form views to kanban cards to systray items — is built as an OWL component extending `Component` from `@odoo/owl` [1][2]. Components declare their templates in static XML files and manage state reactively; when state changes, OWL efficiently re-renders only the affected subtrees. The framework supports props, events (via `trigger` and event handlers), slots for composition, and lifecycle hooks such as `willStart`, `mounted`, `willUnmount`, and `willUpdate` [1][3].
synthesis:40:The Odoo 17 web client uses a **registry-based extension model**. Views (form, list, kanban, pivot, graph, etc.) are registered in `registry.category("views")`, each consisting of a view component, a model class, and a controller. Developers extend or override existing views by wrapping components or patching their templates, rather than subclassing monolithic widget classes [1][3]. Field widgets are similarly registered in `registry.category("fields")` and rendered as OWL components with standard props (`value`, `record`, `onChange`, `readonly`) [1].
synthesis:44:OWL provides React-like **hooks** that encapsulate reusable logic within functional-style component bodies:
synthesis:60:Odoo 17 provides the **`patch`** utility (imported from `@odoo/owl` or `web.utils`) as the primary mechanism for modifying existing components without inheritance. `patch` allows developers to wrap methods, add lifecycle hooks, or modify state on components defined in other modules — a critical capability for Odoo's modular app ecosystem where multiple modules may need to extend the same view [1][3]. This replaces the old `include` mechanism from the Class-based inheritance system and integrates cleanly with OWL's reactivity.
synthesis:64:The Odoo 17 framework includes a **testing infrastructure** based on QUnit with OWL-specific helpers. Tests create components in an isolated test environment with mocked services, use `mount` utilities to render into a test fixture DOM, and can simulate user interactions (clicks, inputs) via helper functions. The `@odoo/owl` test utilities provide `mockService`, `makeTestEnv`, and similar constructs to ensure unit tests are fast and deterministic [1][6].
synthesis:68:For modules migrating from Odoo 15 or earlier, the transition to OWL in Odoo 17 is substantial. The legacy `Widget` base class (`web.Widget`) is fully deprecated. Code must be rewritten as OWL components with ES module imports, QWeb XML templates (rather than JavaScript template definitions), and service-based data access instead of `_rpc` calls. The `Component.extend()` class inheritance pattern is replaced by standard ES class extension and the `patch` function for cross-module modifications [1][3][4]. See [[migration-guide]] for detailed migration strategies.
synthesis:77:    components/     # OWL component .js and .xml files
synthesis:88:Odoo 17's frontend JavaScript architecture represents a mature, opinionated stack built around OWL components, ES modules, services, and registries. Developers coming from React or Vue will find the patterns familiar — reactive state, declarative templates, hooks, and a unidirectional data flow — but must also understand Odoo-specific conventions like the registry system, the `orm` service for RPC, and the `patch` mechanism for cross-module extensibility. Mastery of these four pillars (components, services, registries, and patches) provides the foundation for all frontend development in Odoo 17 [1][2][3][4][5].

| **`buz_pos_promotion`** | Promotion engine — buy X get Y, discount rules, coupon codes, happy hour |
| **`buz_pos_loyalty`** | Loyalty & points — earn/redeem points, tier system, customer history |
| **`buz_pos_retail_ui`** | Retail-optimized UI — quick search, category tiles, product variants grid, customer display |
| **`buz_pos_reports`** | POS analytics — sales dashboard, session summary, product performance, X/Z report |
| **`buz_pos_integration`** | External integrations — receipt printer (ESC/POS), barcode scanner, payment terminal, LINE notify |

from odoo import fields, models


class PosConfig(models.Model):
    _inherit = 'pos.config'

    buz_enable_multi_payment = fields.Boolean(
        string='Allow Multi-Payment',
        default=True,
        help='Allow splitting payment across multiple methods in one order',
    )
    buz_enable_order_note = fields.Boolean(
        string='Allow Order Notes',
        default=True,
    )
    buz_enable_line_discount = fields.Boolean(
        string='Allow Line Discount',
        default=True,
    )
    buz_default_discount = fields.Float(
        string='Default Discount %',
        default=0.0,
    )
    buz_receipt_show_qr = fields.Boolean(
        string='Show QR Code on Receipt',
        default=False,
        help='Display PromptPay QR on receipt for payment',
    )
    buz_promptpay_id = fields.Char(
        string='PromptPay ID (Tax ID or Phone)',
        help='Tax ID or phone number for PromptPay QR generation',
    )
    buz_receipt_footer = fields.Text(

# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

from odoo import fields, models


class ReportSessionSummary(models.AbstractModel):
    _name = 'report.buz_pos_core.report_session_summary'
    _description = 'POS Session Summary Report'

    def _get_report_values(self, docids, data=None):
        session = self.env['pos.session'].browse(docids)
        orders = self.env['pos.order'].search([
            ('session_id', '=', session.id),
            ('state', 'in', ['paid', 'done', 'invoiced']),
        ])

        total_orders = len(orders)
        total_revenue = sum(orders.mapped('amount_total'))
        total_tax = sum(orders.mapped('amount_tax'))
        total_discount = sum(
            sum(line.qty * line.price_unit * (line.discount / 100)
                for line in order.lines)
            for order in orders
        )

        # Payment breakdown
        payment_methods = {}
        for order in orders:
            for payment in order.payment_ids:
                method_name = payment.payment_method_id.name
                payment_methods[method_name] = payment_methods.get(method_name, 0) + payment.amount

        # Source breakdown
        source_breakdown = {}
        for order in orders:
            source = order.buz_source or 'unknown'
            source_breakdown[source] = source_breakdown.get(source, 0) + 1

        # Delivery vs walk-in
        delivery_count = len(orders.filtered(lambda o: o.buz_is_delivery))
        walkin_count = total_orders - delivery_count

        # Top products
        product_sales = {}
        for order in orders:
            for line in order.lines:
                pname = line.product_id.name
                product_sales[pname] = product_sales.get(pname, 0) + line.qty
        top_products = sorted(product_sales.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)[:10]

        return {
            'doc_ids': docids,
            'doc_model': 'pos.session',
            'docs': session,
            'total_orders': total_orders,
            'total_revenue': total_revenue,
            'total_tax': total_tax,
            'total_discount': total_discount,
            'payment_methods': payment_methods,
            'source_breakdown': source_breakdown,
            'delivery_count': delivery_count,
            'walkin_count': walkin_count,
            'top_products': top_products,
        }

@tests.tagged('post_install', '-at_install')
class TestBuzPosCore(tests.TransactionCase):

    def setUp(self):
        super().setUp()
        self.PosConfig = self.env['pos.config']
        self.PosOrder = self.env['pos.order']
        self.Partner = self.env['res.partner']

    def test_pos_config_buz_fields(self):
        """Test that Buz POS config fields are properly stored."""
        config = self.PosConfig.create({
            'name': 'Test Buz POS',
            'buz_enable_multi_payment': True,
            'buz_enable_order_note': True,
            'buz_enable_line_discount': True,
            'buz_receipt_show_qr': True,
            'buz_promptpay_id': '1234567890123',
            'buz_enable_delivery': True,
            'buz_show_stock_in_pos': True,
        })
        self.assertTrue(config.buz_enable_multi_payment)
        self.assertTrue(config.buz_receipt_show_qr)
        self.assertEqual(config.buz_promptpay_id, '1234567890123')
        self.assertTrue(config.buz_enable_delivery)

    def test_partner_driver_field(self):
        """Test driver fields on partner."""
        driver = self.Partner.create({
            'name': 'Test Driver',
            'buz_is_driver': True,
            'buz_driver_license': 'DL12345',
            'buz_driver_vehicle': 'Honda Click',

        self.assertTrue(driver.buz_is_driver)
        self.assertEqual(driver.buz_driver_license, 'DL12345')

    def test_product_buz_fields(self):

        product = self.env['product.product'].create({

{

    'summary': 'Delivery management for Buz POS — zones, drivers, tracking, and fee calculation',
    'description': """
Buz POS Delivery
================
- Delivery zones with distance-based fee
- Driver assignment and status tracking
- Real-time delivery status (pending → assigned → delivering → delivered)
- Auto-create delivery fee line on order
- Delivery address management
- Delivery order list view with kanban
    """,

# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

from odoo import api, fields, models, _
from odoo.exceptions import ValidationError


class DeliveryZone(models.Model):
    _name = 'buz.delivery.zone'
    _description = 'Delivery Zone'
    _order = 'name'

    name = fields.Char(string='Zone Name', required=True)
    company_id = fields.Many2one(
        'res.company', string='Company',
        default=lambda self: self.env.company,
    )
    active = fields.Boolean(default=True)
    delivery_fee = fields.Float(
        string='Delivery Fee', required=True, default=0.0,
        help='Fixed delivery fee for this zone',
    )
    min_order_amount = fields.Float(
        string='Min Order for Free Delivery', default=0.0,
        help='Orders above this amount get free delivery (0 = always charge)',
    )
    estimated_time = fields.Char(
        string='Estimated Delivery Time',
        help='e.g. "15-30 min"',
    )
    radius_km = fields.Float(
        string='Radius (km)',
        help='Delivery radius from shop location',
    )
    color = fields.Integer(string='Color Index', default=0)

    _sql_constraints = [
        ('name_company_uniq', 'unique(name, company_id)', 'Zone name must be unique per company!'),
    ]


class DeliveryDriver(models.Model):
    _name = 'buz.delivery.driver'
    _description = 'Delivery Driver'
    _inherit = ['mail.thread', 'mail.activity.mixin']
    _order = 'name'

    name = fields.Char(string='Driver Name', required=True, tracking=True)
    partner_id = fields.Many2one(
        'res.partner', string='Contact',
        domain="[('buz_is_driver', '=', True)]",
    )
    company_id = fields.Many2one(
        'res.company', default=lambda self: self.env.company,
    )
    active = fields.Boolean(default=True)
    phone = fields.Char(string='Phone', tracking=True)
    vehicle_type = fields.Selection([
        ('motorcycle', 'Motorcycle'),
        ('car', 'Car'),
        ('bicycle', 'Bicycle'),
        ('walk', 'Walk'),
    ], string='Vehicle Type', default='motorcycle')
    vehicle_info = fields.Char(string='Vehicle Info', help='License plate, color, etc.')
    license_no = fields.Char(string='License No.')
    zone_ids = fields.Many2many(
        'buz.delivery.zone', string='Assigned Zones',
        help='Zones this driver can deliver to',
    )
    current_order_id = fields.Many2one(
        'buz.delivery.order', string='Current Delivery',
        compute='_compute_current_order',
    )
    delivery_count = fields.Integer(
        string='Total Deliveries', compute='_compute_delivery_stats',
    )
    state = fields.Selection([
        ('available', 'Available'),
        ('busy', 'On Delivery'),
        ('offline', 'Offline'),
    ], string='Status', default='available', tracking=True)

    @api.depends('state')
    def _compute_current_order(self):
        DeliveryOrder = self.env['buz.delivery.order']
        for driver in self:
            order = DeliveryOrder.search([
                ('driver_id', '=', driver.id),
                ('state', 'in', ['assigned', 'picking', 'delivering']),
            ], limit=1)
            driver.current_order_id = order

    def _compute_delivery_stats(self):
        DeliveryOrder = self.env['buz.delivery.order']
        for driver in self:
            driver.delivery_count = DeliveryOrder.search_count([
                ('driver_id', '=', driver.id),
                ('state', '=', 'delivered'),
            ])


class DeliveryOrder(models.Model):
    _name = 'buz.delivery.order'
    _description = 'Delivery Order'
    _inherit = ['mail.thread', 'mail.activity.mixin']
    _order = 'create_date desc'

    name = fields.Char(
        string='Reference', required=True, readonly=True,
        default=lambda self: _('New'),
    )
    company_id = fields.Many2one(
        'res.company', default=lambda self: self.env.company,
    )
    pos_order_id = fields.Many2one(
        'pos.order', string='POS Order', readonly=True,
    )
    partner_id = fields.Many2one(
        'res.partner', string='Customer',
    )
    driver_id = fields.Many2one(
        'buz.delivery.driver', string='Driver', tracking=True,
    )
    zone_id = fields.Many2one(
        'buz.delivery.zone', string='Delivery Zone',
    )
    state = fields.Selection([
        ('pending', 'Pending'),
        ('assigned', 'Driver Assigned'),
        ('picking', 'Picking Up'),
        ('delivering', 'Out for Delivery'),
        ('delivered', 'Delivered'),
        ('failed', 'Delivery Failed'),
        ('cancelled', 'Cancelled'),
    ], string='Status', default='pending', tracking=True)
    delivery_address = fields.Text(string='Delivery Address', required=True)
    delivery_phone = fields.Char(string='Contact Phone', required=True)
    delivery_fee = fields.Float(string='Delivery Fee', default=0.0)
    scheduled_date = fields.Datetime(string='Scheduled Delivery')
    delivered_date = fields.Datetime(string='Delivered At')
    note = fields.Text(string='Delivery Notes')
    fail_reason = fields.Text(string='Failure Reason')

    @api.model_create_multi
    def create(self, vals_list):
        for vals in vals_list:
            if vals.get('name', _('New')) == _('New'):
                vals['name'] = self.env['ir.sequence'].next_by_code(
                    'buz.delivery.order'
                ) or _('New')
        return super().create(vals_list)

    def action_assign_driver(self):
        self.ensure_one()
        if self.driver_id:
            self.driver_id.state = 'busy'
        self.state = 'assigned'

    def action_start_picking(self):
        self.ensure_one()
        self.state = 'picking'

    def action_start_delivery(self):
        self.ensure_one()
        self.state = 'delivering'

    def action_delivered(self):
        self.ensure_one()
        self.state = 'delivered'
        self.delivered_date = fields.Datetime.now()
        if self.driver_id:
            self.driver_id.state = 'available'
        # Update POS order delivery status
        if self.pos_order_id:
            self.pos_order_id.buz_delivery_status = 'delivered'

    def action_fail(self):
        self.ensure_one()
        self.state = 'failed'
        if self.driver_id:
            self.driver_id.state = 'available'

    def action_cancel(self):
        self.ensure_one()
        self.state = 'cancelled'
        if self.driver_id:
            self.driver_id.state = 'available'

    def action_reset_to_pending(self):
        self.ensure_one()
        self.state = 'pending'
        self.driver_id = False

{
    'name': 'Buz POS Promotion',
    'version': '17.0.1.0.0',
    'category': 'Point Of Sale',
    'summary': 'Promotion engine for Buz POS — discount rules, buy X get Y, coupon codes',
    'description': """
Buz POS Promotion
=================
- Discount rules (% or fixed amount)
- Buy X Get Y promotions
- Happy hour / time-based promotions
- Coupon codes with usage limits
- Auto-apply or manual code entry
    """,
    'author': 'Buz',
    'license': 'LGPL-3',
    'depends': ['buz_pos_core'],
    'data': [
        'security/security.xml',
        'security/ir.model.access.csv',
        'data/sequence_data.xml',
        'views/promotion_rule_views.xml',
        'views/promotion_coupon_views.xml',

    ],

        'point_of_sale._assets_pos': [
            'buz_pos_promotion/static/src/css/promotion.css',

            'buz_pos_promotion/static/src/js/PromotionEngine.js',
            'buz_pos_promotion/static/src/xml/PromotionPopup.xml',
        ],
    },
    'application': True,
    'installable': True,
    'auto_install': False,
}

# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

from odoo import api, fields, models, _
from odoo.exceptions import ValidationError


class PromotionRule(models.Model):
    _name = 'buz.promotion.rule'
    _description = 'Promotion Rule'
    _order = 'sequence, id'

    name = fields.Char(string='Promotion Name', required=True)
    sequence = fields.Integer(default=10)
    company_id = fields.Many2one(
        'res.company', default=lambda self: self.env.company,
    )
    active = fields.Boolean(default=True)

    # Rule type
    rule_type = fields.Selection([
        ('discount_percent', 'Discount %'),
        ('discount_fixed', 'Fixed Amount Discount'),
        ('buy_x_get_y', 'Buy X Get Y'),
        ('buy_x_get_discount', 'Buy X Get Discount'),
    ], string='Type', required=True, default='discount_percent')

    # Conditions
    min_order_amount = fields.Float(
        string='Min Order Amount', default=0.0,
        help='Minimum order total to trigger promotion',
    )
    product_ids = fields.Many2many(
        'product.product', string='Applicable Products',
        help='Leave empty for all products',
    )
    category_ids = fields.Many2many(
        'pos.category', string='Applicable Categories',
        help='Leave empty for all categories',
    )
    min_qty = fields.Integer(
        string='Min Quantity', default=0,
        help='Minimum quantity of applicable products',
    )

    # Discount settings
    discount_percent = fields.Float(string='Discount %')
    discount_fixed_amount = fields.Float(string='Fixed Discount Amount')
    discount_product_id = fields.Many2one(
        'product.product', string='Discount Product',
        help='Product used as discount line in POS',
    )

    # Buy X Get Y settings
    buy_product_id = fields.Many2one(
        'product.product', string='Buy Product',
    )
    buy_qty = fields.Integer(string='Buy Quantity', default=1)
    get_product_id = fields.Many2one(
        'product.product', string='Get Product',
    )
    get_qty = fields.Integer(string='Get Quantity', default=1)
    get_discount = fields.Float(
        string='Discount on Get Product %', default=100,
        help='100% = free, 50% = half price',
    )

    # Time-based
    is_time_based = fields.Boolean(string='Time-based', default=False)
    start_time = fields.Float(string='Start Hour', default=0.0)
    end_time = fields.Float(string='End Hour', default=24.0)
    day_of_week = fields.Selection([
        ('0', 'Monday'),
        ('1', 'Tuesday'),
        ('2', 'Wednesday'),
        ('3', 'Thursday'),
        ('4', 'Friday'),
        ('5', 'Saturday'),
        ('6', 'Sunday'),
    ], string='Day of Week')

    # Usage limits
    max_use = fields.Integer(string='Max Usage', default=0, help='0 = unlimited')
    use_count = fields.Integer(string='Usage Count', default=0, compute='_compute_use_count')

    # Auto-apply
    auto_apply = fields.Boolean(
        string='Auto Apply', default=True,
        help='Automatically apply when conditions met, otherwise require coupon code',
    )

    _sql_constraints = [
        ('name_company_uniq', 'unique(name, company_id)', 'Promotion name must be unique per company!'),
    ]

    def _compute_use_count(self):
        Coupon = self.env['buz.promotion.coupon']
        for rule in self:
            rule.use_count = Coupon.search_count([
                ('rule_id', '=', rule.id),
                ('state', '=', 'used'),
            ])

    def _is_active_now(self):
        """Check if time-based promotion is currently active."""
        if not self.is_time_based:
            return True
        now = fields.Datetime.now()
        current_hour = now.hour + now.minute / 60.0
        current_day = str(now.weekday())

        if self.day_of_week and self.day_of_week != current_day:
            return False
        if self.start_time <= current_hour <= self.end_time:
            return True
        return False


class PromotionCoupon(models.Model):
    _name = 'buz.promotion.coupon'
    _description = 'Promotion Coupon'

    name = fields.Char(
        string='Coupon Code', required=True,
        default=lambda self: self.env['ir.sequence'].next_by_code('buz.promotion.coupon') or _('New'),
    )
    rule_id = fields.Many2one(
        'buz.promotion.rule', string='Promotion Rule', required=True,
    )
    company_id = fields.Many2one(
        'res.company', default=lambda self: self.env.company,
    )
    state = fields.Selection([
        ('draft', 'Draft'),
        ('active', 'Active'),
        ('used', 'Used'),
        ('expired', 'Expired'),
    ], default='active', string='Status')
    partner_id = fields.Many2one(
        'res.partner', string='Customer',
        help='Leave empty for any customer',
    )
    max_use = fields.Integer(string='Max Use', default=1)
    use_count = fields.Integer(string='Use Count', default=0)
    expiry_date = fields.Date(string='Expiry Date')
    pos_order_id = fields.Many2one('pos.order', string='POS Order')
    note = fields.Text(string='Note')

    @api.model_create_multi
    def create(self, vals_list):
        for vals in vals_list:
            if vals.get('name', _('New')) == _('New'):
                vals['name'] = self.env['ir.sequence'].next_by_code(
                    'buz.promotion.coupon'
                ) or _('New')
        return super().create(vals_list)

    def action_use(self):
        self.ensure_one()
        if self.use_count >= self.max_use:
            raise ValidationError(_('Coupon has reached max usage.'))
        self.use_count += 1
        if self.use_count >= self.max_use:
            self.state = 'used'

    def action_reset(self):
        self.ensure_one()
        self.state = 'active'
        self.use_count = 0

    'category': 'Point Of Sale',
    'summary': 'POS analytics — X-report, Z-report, session summary, product ranking, payment breakdown',
    'description': """
Buz POS Reports
===============
- X-Report (mid-session summary without closing)
- Z-Report (end-of-session final report)
- Product ranking by qty sold and revenue
- Payment method breakdown
- Hourly sales analysis
- Source-based analysis (walk-in, phone, LINE, online)
    """,
    'author': 'Buz',

    'data': [
        'report/x_report.xml',
        'report/z_report.xml',

        'views/pos_session_views.xml',

    ],
    'assets': {},
    'application': True,
    'installable': True,
    'auto_install': False,
}

# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

from odoo import fields, models


class PosSession(models.Model):
    _inherit = 'pos.session'

    buz_x_report_count = fields.Integer(
        string='X-Report Count', default=0,
    )

# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

from odoo import fields, models


class ReportXReport(models.AbstractModel):
    _name = 'report.buz_pos_reports.x_report'
    _description = 'X-Report (Mid-Session)'

    def _get_report_values(self, docids, data=None):
        session = self.env['pos.session'].browse(docids)
        orders = self.env['pos.order'].search([
            ('session_id', '=', session.id),
            ('state', 'in', ['paid', 'done', 'invoiced']),
        ])

        total_orders = len(orders)
        total_revenue = sum(orders.mapped('amount_total'))
        total_tax = sum(orders.mapped('amount_tax'))
        total_untaxed = sum(orders.mapped('amount_untaxed'))

        # Payment breakdown
        payment_methods = {}
        for order in orders:
            for payment in order.payment_ids:
                method_name = payment.payment_method_id.name
                payment_methods[method_name] = payment_methods.get(method_name, 0) + payment.amount

        # Hourly breakdown
        hourly = {}
        for order in orders:
            if order.date_order:
                hour = fields.Datetime.context_timestamp(
                    self, order.date_order
                ).strftime('%H:00')
                hourly[hour] = hourly.get(hour, {'count': 0, 'revenue': 0})
                hourly[hour]['count'] += 1
                hourly[hour]['revenue'] += order.amount_total

        # Source breakdown
        source_breakdown = {}
        for order in orders:
            source = order.buz_source or 'unknown'
            source_breakdown.setdefault(source, {'count': 0, 'revenue': 0})
            source_breakdown[source]['count'] += 1
            source_breakdown[source]['revenue'] += order.amount_total

        return {
            'doc_ids': docids,
            'doc_model': 'pos.session',
            'docs': session,
            'total_orders': total_orders,
            'total_revenue': total_revenue,
            'total_tax': total_tax,
            'total_untaxed': total_untaxed,
            'payment_methods': payment_methods,
            'hourly': hourly,
            'source_breakdown': source_breakdown,
            'print_time': fields.Datetime.now(),
        }

# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

from odoo import fields, models


class ReportZReport(models.AbstractModel):
    _name = 'report.buz_pos_reports.z_report'
    _description = 'Z-Report (End-of-Session)'

    def _get_report_values(self, docids, data=None):
        session = self.env['pos.session'].browse(docids)
        orders = self.env['pos.order'].search([
            ('session_id', '=', session.id),
            ('state', 'in', ['paid', 'done', 'invoiced']),
        ])

        total_orders = len(orders)
        total_revenue = sum(orders.mapped('amount_total'))
        total_tax = sum(orders.mapped('amount_tax'))

        # Payment breakdown
        payment_methods = {}
        for order in orders:
            for payment in order.payment_ids:
                method_name = payment.payment_method_id.name
                payment_methods[method_name] = payment_methods.get(method_name, 0) + payment.amount

        # Delivery vs walk-in
        delivery_count = len(orders.filtered(lambda o: o.buz_is_delivery))
        delivery_revenue = sum(
            orders.filtered(lambda o: o.buz_is_delivery).mapped('amount_total')
        )

        # Top products
        product_sales = {}
        for order in orders:
            for line in order.lines:
                pname = line.product_id.name
                if pname not in product_sales:
                    product_sales[pname] = {'qty': 0, 'revenue': 0}
                product_sales[pname]['qty'] += line.qty
                product_sales[pname]['revenue'] += line.price_subtotal_incl
        top_products = sorted(
            product_sales.items(), key=lambda x: x[1]['revenue'], reverse=True
        )[:20]

        # Discounts
        total_discount = sum(
            sum(line.qty * line.price_unit * (line.discount / 100)
                for line in order.lines)
            for order in orders
        )

        # Refunds
        refund_orders = orders.filtered(lambda o: o.amount_total < 0)
        total_refund = abs(sum(refund_orders.mapped('amount_total')))

        return {
            'doc_ids': docids,
            'doc_model': 'pos.session',
            'docs': session,
            'total_orders': total_orders,
            'total_revenue': total_revenue,
            'total_tax': total_tax,
            'total_discount': total_discount,
            'total_refund': total_refund,
            'payment_methods': payment_methods,
            'delivery_count': delivery_count,
            'delivery_revenue': delivery_revenue,
            'top_products': top_products,
            'print_time': fields.Datetime.now(),
        }

# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
from odoo import tests


@tests.tagged('post_install', '-at_install')
class TestBuzDelivery(tests.TransactionCase):

    def setUp(self):
        super().setUp()
        self.Zone = self.env['buz.delivery.zone']
        self.Driver = self.env['buz.delivery.driver']
        self.DeliveryOrder = self.env['buz.delivery.order']

    def test_create_zone(self):
        zone = self.Zone.create({
            'name': 'Zone A (0-5km)',
            'delivery_fee': 30.0,
            'estimated_time': '15-30 min',
            'radius_km': 5.0,
        })
        self.assertEqual(zone.name, 'Zone A (0-5km)')
        self.assertEqual(zone.delivery_fee, 30.0)

    def test_create_driver(self):
        driver = self.Driver.create({
            'name': 'สมชาย คนส่ง',
            'phone': '0812345678',
            'vehicle_type': 'motorcycle',
            'vehicle_info': 'Honda Click 125',
        })
        self.assertEqual(driver.state, 'available')
        self.assertEqual(driver.vehicle_type, 'motorcycle')

    def test_delivery_order_workflow(self):
        zone = self.Zone.create({'name': 'Test Zone', 'delivery_fee': 20.0})
        driver = self.Driver.create({'name': 'Driver 1', 'phone': '0899999999'})
        order = self.DeliveryOrder.create({
            'delivery_address': '123 Test Road',
            'delivery_phone': '0811111111',
            'zone_id': zone.id,
            'driver_id': driver.id,
        })
        self.assertEqual(order.state, 'pending')
        self.assertTrue(order.name.startswith('DLV'))

        order.action_assign_driver()
        self.assertEqual(order.state, 'assigned')
        self.assertEqual(driver.state, 'busy')

        order.action_start_picking()
        self.assertEqual(order.state, 'picking')

        order.action_start_delivery()
        self.assertEqual(order.state, 'delivering')

        order.action_delivered()
        self.assertEqual(order.state, 'delivered')
        self.assertEqual(driver.state, 'available')

        })
        partner = self.Partner.create({'name': 'Test Customer'})
        card = self.Card.create({'partner_id': partner.id})

        # Should be Bronze initially
        self.assertEqual(card.tier_id.id, bronze.id)

        # Add points to reach Silver
        card.add_points(150, description='Test purchase')

        # Tier should auto-upgrade
        self.assertEqual(card.tier_id.id, silver.id)
        self.assertEqual(card.total_points, 150)
        self.assertEqual(card.available_points, 150)

    def test_redeem_points(self):
        partner = self.Partner.create({'name': 'Redeem Customer'})
        card = self.Card.create({'partner_id': partner.id})
        card.add_points(100, description='Earn')

        result = card.redeem_points(50, description='Redeem')
        self.assertTrue(result)
        self.assertEqual(card.available_points, 50)
        self.assertEqual(card.total_points, 100)

        # Cannot redeem more than available
        result = card.redeem_points(100, description='Over redeem')
        self.assertFalse(result)

input.content:
---
title: Buz POS Ecosystem Architecture
topic: Full-featured Retail POS system for Odoo 17 with delivery, promotions, loyalty, retail UI, reports, and integrations
sources: ["session-pos-ecosystem-dev"]
category: pos
tags: [Odoo17, POS, Retail, Delivery, OWL, frontend]
---

## Overview

Buz POS เป็นระบบ Point-of-Sale แบบ full-featured สำหรับธุรกิจ Retail บน Odoo 17 ออกแบบเป็น modular ecosystem 7 modules:

## Module Dependency Graph

```
point_of_sale (Odoo Core)
    └── buz_pos_core (base + OWL components)
        ├── buz_pos_delivery (delivery management)
        ├── buz_pos_promotion (discount rules + coupons)
        ├── buz_pos_loyalty (points + tiers)
        ├── buz_pos_retail_ui (retail-optimized UI)
        ├── buz_pos_reports (X/Z report + analytics)
        └── buz_pos_integration (hardware + LINE + PromptPay)
```

## Modules

### 1. buz_pos_core
**Base POS enhancement**
- Extend `pos.config` with settings: multi-payment, order notes, line discount, QR receipt, delivery toggle, stock display
- Extend `pos.order` with: order note, delivery info, source tracking, delivery status
- Extend `pos.order.line` with: line note, discount reason
- OWL frontend: ProductScreen patch, PaymentScreen patch, ReceiptScreen patch, OrderWidget patch, models.js patch
- CSS: Touchscreen-optimized retail UI
- Report: Session Summary PDF

### 2. buz_pos_delivery
**Delivery management**
- Models: `buz.delivery.zone` (zone + fee), `buz.delivery.driver` (driver + vehicle + status), `buz.delivery.order` (full delivery workflow)
- Workflow: pending → assigned → picking → delivering → delivered
- Driver status tracking (available/busy/offline)
- OWL: DeliveryPopup (address, phone, zone, fee calc), DeliveryScreen button
- Auto-create delivery order when POS order confirmed
- Kanban board for delivery dashboard

### 3. buz_pos_promotion
**Promotion engine**
- Models: `buz.promotion.rule` (discount %, fixed, buy X get Y, time-based), `buz.promotion.coupon` (code + usage tracking)
- Time-based promotions (happy hour, specific day)
- Auto-apply or coupon-based
- OWL: PromotionPopup (coupon code input), PromotionEngine (auto-apply logic)

### 4. buz_pos_loyalty
**Loyalty & points**
- Models: `buz.loyalty.tier` (Bronze/Silver/Gold/Platinum), `buz.loyalty.card` (points tracking), `buz.loyalty.transaction` (earn/redeem/adjust)
- Auto tier upgrade based on accumulated points
- Default tiers: Bronze (0), Silver (500), Gold (2000), Platinum (5000)
- Points earn/redeem on POS order completion
- OWL: LoyaltyWidget (points display), RedeemPopup (redeem points as discount)

### 5. buz_pos_retail_ui
**Retail-optimized frontend**
- Category Tiles: Touch-friendly category navigation (small/medium/large)
- QuickSearchBar: Instant product search with barcode support
- ProductVariantGrid: Variant selector popup
- CustomerPanel: Customer-facing display (dark theme)
- Config: tile size, show images, show price, show stock badge, customer display toggle

### 6. buz_pos_reports
**Analytics & reports**
- X-Report: Mid-session summary (orders, revenue, payment breakdown, hourly, source)
- Z-Report: End-of-session final (revenue, discounts, refunds, delivery, top products)
- Product Ranking: By revenue and by quantity
- All reports as QWeb PDF
- Buttons on POS Session form

### 7. buz_pos_integration
**Hardware & external**
- ESC/POS: Network thermal printer connection (IP + port), receipt command builder (58mm/80mm)
- Barcode Scanner: Keyboard event listener (auto-detect rapid keystrokes), product lookup
- PromptPay QR: EMVCo format QR payload generation with CRC16
- LINE Notify: Order notification via LINE OA, delivery status notification

## Key Technical Decisions

- **OWL `patch` mechanism**: All frontend extensions use `patch()` instead of class inheritance
- **`_inherit` for models**: Extend existing Odoo models with `_inherit`
- **`export_as_JSON` / `init_from_JSON`**: Sync custom fields between frontend and backend
- **Multi-company**: All models have `company_id` + record rules
- **Security**: ACL per user/manager group, record rules for company isolation
- **Touch-friendly**: All buttons min 44px, large touch targets, responsive grid

## Data Models Summary

| Model | Module | Purpose |
|-------|--------|---------|
| pos.config (+) | core | POS settings |
| pos.order (+) | core/delivery/promotion/loyalty | Order with delivery, promo, loyalty |
| pos.order.line (+) | core | Line notes, discount reasons |
| res.partner (+) | core | Driver info, loyalty points |
| product.product (+) | core | POS sequence, best seller, color |
| buz.delivery.zone | delivery | Zone + fee |
| buz.delivery.driver | delivery | Driver + vehicle + status |
| buz.delivery.order | delivery | Full delivery tracking |
| buz.promotion.rule | promotion | Promotion rules |
| buz.promotion.coupon | promotion | Coupon codes |
| buz.loyalty.tier | loyalty | Tier levels |
| buz.loyalty.card | loyalty | Customer loyalty card |
| buz.loyalty.transaction | loyalty | Point transactions |

## File Statistics

- 7 modules created
- ~60+ files total
- Backend: Python models, views, security, reports
- Frontend: OWL JS components, QWeb XML templates, CSS
